title: Connect to a Wi-Fi network in Windows
license_note: Screenshots from support.microsoft.com. Confirm redistribution with your legal team before use in a shipped product.

steps.text: Select the Network , Sound , or Battery icons ( ) on the taskbar to open quick settings. The Disconnected icon appears when you’re not connected.
steps.caption: The Network Offline icon in Windows 11.

steps.text: On the Wi-Fi quick setting, select Manage Wi-Fi connections .
steps.caption: Where to find "Manage Wi-Fi connections" in the Windows 11 quick settings.

steps.text: Choose the Wi-Fi network you want, then select Connect . If you don't see the network you expect, you can manually start a new Wi-Fi scan by selecting the Refresh network list ​​​ button in the lower right.
Type the network password, and then select Next .

sections.title: Connecting to a Wi-Fi network with a QR code

steps.text: Open the Camera app. Select the barcode mode icon to switch from photo mode to barcode mode.
steps.caption: The barcode mode icon in the Windows 11 Camera app.

steps.text: Point your device's camera at the QR code that contains the Wi-Fi connection information.
Select the link that appears in the Camera app, which will open Settings .
In Settings, confirm that you want to add and connect to the Wi-Fi network. You can choose if your device will connect automatically to that network when it is in range.
steps.caption: Adding a Wi-Fi network to Windows 11.

steps.text: Use random hardware addresses for all networks In the Settings app on your Windows device, select Network & internet > Wi-Fi . Turn on Random hardware addresses .

Use random hardware addresses for a specific network In the Settings app on your Windows device, select Network & internet > Wi-Fi > Manage known networks . Choose a Wi-Fi network, then turn on Random hardware addresses .
